Strip_Summary: Stickman on sofa in dark suburban living room. TV showing capital city burning. Blue TV glow. Cold tea on side table.
Stickman on sofa ten thousand km away. Glass of cold tea. Wife made it two hours ago. American suburb. TV still showing war.
First-person view from sofa. TV showing bombs like fireworks from distance. Country being bombed. Not there.
Stickman on sofa. Haven't been there since seventeen. Forty-seven years watching from outside.
Weight line: You lost a country before you were old enough to understand what it was.
Child stickman age seven in imperial palace. High ceilings echo voice. Marble floors cold under bare feet. Rose gardens with guards.
Guards saluting as child stickman walks past. Cannot tell their faces apart. Palace life.
Child stickman in miniature military uniform on national holiday. Gold buttons. Tiny sword. Waving from balcony. Crowd waving back.
Father rules 2500-year dynasty. Kings and conquerors. Child stickman is next link in chain.
Seventeen. Sent abroad for pilot training. Mother holds his face at airport. Engine noise drowns her words.
Packing bag. Not looking back at palace. Doesn't know it's the last time. Back in a year. Everyone wrong.
Revolution happens while across ocean. Hearing about it on TV in foreign dormitory.
Family flees. Palace goes dark. Reopens as museum of dynasty crimes  -  admission free.
Bedroom now exhibit. Toys behind glass. Name on caption. Visitors take photos.
Trying to call home. Line dead. Trying next day. And the next. Can't go back.
Weight line: No one can go back.
Twenty. Father dies in borrowed palace in borrowed city. Cancer hidden from family.
Ruled dynasty older than most civilizations. Died in room not his. City offered shelter out of pity.
Reading condolence telegrams in rented apartment. Twenty years old. Heir to nothing.
Declaring successor to throne that doesn't exist. Small room. No crown. Just tape recorder and traffic.
Years pass. Suburb. Marriage. Three daughters. Press conferences no one in country can hear.
Sister dies in London hotel. Thirty-one. Overdose. Hotel finds her.
Exile found crack in her first. Youngest. Remembered least. Nothing she remembered still destroyed her.
Brother dies by suicide. Forty-four. Intellectual. Philosopher of identity and exile.
Male line ends. Two thousand five hundred years stops in Boston bedroom on Tuesday afternoon.
Weight: Exile doesn't kill with bullets. It kills with distance. And patience. And quiet rooms.
Decades pass. Suburb outside capital of asylum country. Mowing lawn Saturdays. Watching country on TV.
Economic protests erupt. First time in forty years  -  chant old dynasty name. You.
Your name. In country you haven't touched in half a lifetime.
2022. Women cut hair in street. Hold braids like flags. Largest uprising in generation.
Girl dies in custody. Country ignites. Your name on every wall and every breath.
Name chanted in streets not walked since seven. By people born after he left. Children whose grandparents remember father's secret police.
Watching videos on phone. In bed. 2am. Wife breathing beside. Blue phone glow only thing moving in house.
Weight line: They call your name as if you could save them.
Regime strikes back. Hundreds arrested. Executions. Streets go quiet  -  louder than any crowd.
Videos stop coming. Accounts go dark. DMs unanswered. Silence worse than screaming.
Recording video from American studio. 4K camera. Professional microphone. Old flag behind.
Speaking directly to camera. Voice doesn't shake  -  practiced in front of mirror until it stopped.
Watching playback. Looking presidential. Wondering when that became the goal.
Internet cut inside country. Nobody can see video. Shouting into room that can't hear.
Meeting Western politicians. Handshakes in marble hallways. Photo ops. Promises of support.
Nothing changes. Meetings keep happening. Handshakes keep happening. Country keeps burning.
Speaking at conservative forum. Standing ovation. Audience loves imagined version of country.
Back home calling him Western puppet. Never heard the speech. Don't need to.
Exiled opposition splits. Republicans hate title. Monarchists hate refusal of throne.
Too royal for democrats. Too democratic for royals. Late night wife finds him in dark living room. TV on mute.
She stands in doorway. Seen this before. He knows exactly what's needed: coalition, strike, international pressure.
Weight: No army. No territory. No leverage that isn't borrowed.
Symbol of freedom. Can't open front door without checking security camera. Hand reaches for lock. Fingers tremble then grip hard.
February 2026. Allied missiles hit capital first time. Sky lit white over city where he was born.
Watching live on cable news. In living room. Daughters' school photos on wall behind screen.
Same streets from armored car at five. Mother's hand. Tinted glass. People waving or running.
Camera shows old district. Recognizes intersection. Or thinks so. Everything looks different when on fire.
On American TV. Calling it humanitarian intervention. In English.
Anchor asks how it feels. Gives prepared answer. Not the one he feels.
Not even close. Forty-seven years. Knows which face for which camera.
Asks to spare infrastructure. Water plants. Hospitals. Bridges. Things keeping three million alive.
That belongs to my people. Says it like someone who still owns the place.
Week three. Hospital hit. School collapses. Southern water supply fails. Three hundred thousand boil river water.
Posts about it. Six hundred retweets. Twelve call him traitor. One says he called bombs himself.
Week five. Records video. Tells people to stay home. Wait for safety.
Wait for final call. The one that tells them when to take back streets.
Final call never comes. Drafted three times on phone. Deleted three times.
Doesn't know when to make it. Ten thousand km away watching on screen with two-second delay to everything  -  including his relevance.
Regime fractures but doesn't fall. Not cleanly. Not like imagined from suburban bedroom smelling like laundry detergent.
Inside country: some chant name like prayer. Others burn father's portrait. Tyrant, parasite, relic.
Standing before Western parliament. Speaking about homeland in foreign language. Audience will forget name by next week.
Polite applause. Photo afterward. Both know photo will never be posted.
Phone buzzes. Video from inside country. Children on hospital floor. No beds. Nurse covers child with own coat. Hand shaking. Child doesn't move.
Watching three times. Four times. Thumb hovering over share button. Doesn't press it.
Everything wanted for forty-seven years is happening. Screens flash, sirens, daughter's footsteps. Everything blurs. Blinks. Back.
Weight line: And it looks nothing like any dream you ever had.
3am. Wife and daughters asleep upstairs. Stickman sitting downstairs. TV still on. Always on.
Six screens on news channel. Split down middle. Bombs left. Interview face right.
Watching himself speaking about country while it burns behind his head. Daughter comes downstairs.
Dad, are you okay? In English. She asks it in English.
Three daughters speak English to each other. At dinner. In car. In arguments. Use his language only to call him.
Language dying inside own house. Syllable by syllable. Like a leak you can't find.
Named daughters as heirs. Broke 2500 years of male succession. Not equality  -  brother dead, line died with him.
House listed eight million. Four bedrooms. Pool. American flag on neighbor's porch. Rich by every standard except own.
Weight: Homeless by your own.
Every interview same question. Do you want to be king. Ten thousand times. Says no. Still carries title.
Maybe keeps it from duty. Maybe without it just a man on sofa watching news. Stopped figuring out which.
Calling mother. Eighty-six. Clear voice. Same question every week since 1979.
When are we going home. Doesn't answer. She doesn't need him to.
Looking at hands. Pilot's hands. Trained for combat jets. Top marks. Best reflexes.
Never flew a single mission. Not one. Hands just sit in lap now.
Breaking news. Capital lost power. Three million in dark. Candles in windows like vigil.
Forty-seven years ago palace burned bright every night. Remembers light. Doesn't remember smell of streets.
Built whole world for children here. Schools. Friends. Birthday parties. Mailman knows name not title.
Weight line: But you still live there  -  on the screen.
Living room. TV on mute. Capital is dark now. Mirror of HOOK.
Seventeen when you left. One bag. Mother said something at gate. Couldn't hear over engine. Never asked.
Sixty-five. Still here. On sofa. In suburb. Daughters learned bikes here and forgot language here.
Weight: You don't know who you are without that country. You've never known.
Final frame. TV turns off. Screen goes black. Stickman reflection in dark TV glass. Alone.
